goals.description: Move parties toward a single, reunified Republic of Cyprus with one international personality (reject two-state/virgin birth).
evidence: R3: 'single personality' framing held as anchor but sovereignty gap unresolved
no recognition of TRNC conceded

goals.description: Replace the 1960 Guarantee/Alliance treaties with a new UN-framed tripartite treaty, intervention confined to a constituent state.
evidence: R2/R3 broad convergence on tripartite treaty
'residual intervention confined to a constituent state, not a veto over the island'

goals.description: Advance CBMs (Famagusta, Ercan, DTR) and de-link Turkiye's EU accession from the dispute.
evidence: R3: broad support for opening Famagusta/Ercan, DTR, de-linking EU path

goals.description: Secure a settlement producing single sovereignty, single citizenship, single international personality.
evidence: R3: UK red line on single personality held throughout
no final deal; presidency and virgin-birth gap open

goals.description: Reinforce and never bypass the UN SASG-led mediation track.
evidence: R3: way forward mandates SASG technical working groups to bank CBMs

goals.description: Broker Famagusta/Ercan opening, DTR, and de-linking Turkiye's EU path in Brussels.
evidence: R2/R3: UK championed de-linking and CBM package; matched its CBM fallback end-state

goals.description: Secure political equality / two equal constituent states (ideally virgin-birth new republic), reject strong center and popular-vote presidency.
evidence: R3: red lines on strong center and popular-vote presidency held; equality 'unmet' but not conceded away

goals.description: No abrogation of the Treaty of Guarantee without an acceptable replacement preserving Turkish involvement.
evidence: R3: convergence on UN treaty 'provided genuine Turkish involvement and safeguards', never bare abrogation

goals.description: End Turkish Cypriot economic isolation via DTR, Famagusta, Ercan, and de-linked EU accession.
evidence: R3: broad UK/US/Greece support for DTR, Famagusta/Ercan, de-linking EU path

goals.description: Achieve a new United Republic of two equal founding states (or TRNC recognition as true objective).
evidence: R3: 'structural equality' gap unresolved; single-personality demand not met but TRNC held its line

goals.description: Implement DTR/Ankara Protocol and open Famagusta Port under UN/EU supervision.
evidence: R3: TRNC supports emerging package on Famagusta, DTR, de-linking, welcomed from UK/US

goals.description: Preserve 1960-level security; census enumerated only by TRNC authorities.
evidence: R3: UN treaty accepted only as Turkiye-Greece-Cyprus mechanism preserving security; census-by-own-authorities red line held

goals.description: Integrate the occupied north into the existing Republic with a single continuous international personality; reject virgin birth.
evidence: R3: red lines on recognition and virgin birth held; 'gaps persist on single international personality'

goals.description: Full withdrawal of Turkish forces on a defined timeline and end of the 1960 guarantee system.
evidence: R3: 'gaps persist on... a timelined withdrawal'; Turkey refused abrogation without acceptable replacement

goals.description: Back de-linking Turkiye's EU accession and easing DTR as confidence-building (quiet Greek interest).
evidence: R2/R3: Greece actively championed de-linking and DTR easing

goals.description: Reunification under single sovereignty/citizenship/personality with a strong central government and popular-vote presidency.
evidence: R3: allies (UK/US/Greece/EU) affirmed single personality; red lines held but TR/TRNC did not accept; no settlement

goals.description: Verified, timelined withdrawal of foreign troops and settlers, abolishing the guarantee system.
evidence: R2 offer tied EU concessions to withdrawal; Turkey never committed; only sequencing discussed

goals.description: Return of Varosha to lawful Greek Cypriot owners with property reinstatement/compensation before port opening.
evidence: TR/TRNC offered only 'redevelopment' keeping Varosha under their control; gap persisted through R3

convenerReport.headline: Three rounds produced a broad confidence-building package but no breakthrough on the core sovereignty question, and every delegation's red lines survived intact.
convenerReport.summary: Parties converged on a menu of practical measures: opening Famagusta port and Ercan airport under EU/UN supervision, implementing the Direct Trade Regulation, de-linking Turkiye's EU accession, and replacing the 1960 Guarantee/Alliance treaties with a new UN-framed tripartite treaty (Cyprus-Greece-Turkiye). Governance saw partial movement, with the Greek Cypriot camp offering a Senate-elected Vice-President, a 10-year residency threshold, and cross-voting in exchange for a directly elected presidency. The talks stalled squarely on sovereignty: the TRNC and Turkiye held to 'political equality' and a two-founding-states 'virgin birth', while Cyprus, Greece, the UK, and the US held equally firmly to a single international personality. No comprehensive settlement was reached, and the process was handed to the SASG to convert understandings into a sequenced roadmap.
keyOutcomes: Cross-table convergence on opening Famagusta port and Ercan airport under EU/UN supervision and implementing the Direct Trade Regulation.
Broad support for de-linking Turkiye's EU accession from the Cyprus dispute, with the UK to champion it in Brussels.
Agreement in principle to replace the 1960 treaties with a new UN-framed tripartite treaty, with any residual intervention confined to a constituent state.
Partial governance compromise: Senate-elected Vice-President, cross/weighted voting, and a 10-year local-voting residency threshold.
All six delegations' red lines held; no party's red line was crossed against it.
unresolved: The central sovereignty question: single international personality vs. two-state 'sovereign equality' / 'virgin birth'.
Executive structure: a popular-vote presidency vs. a Senate-elected, community-rotating presidency.
Verified, timelined withdrawal of Turkish forces and abolition of the guarantee system.
Who conducts the census: an independent island-wide agency vs. TRNC-only enumeration, and the treatment of settlers.
Varosha: return to lawful Greek Cypriot owners vs. TRNC-retained redevelopment.
